text: Flag when total deal-level fees exceed 10% of effective gross revenues
Highlight DSCR below 1.0x as critical debt coverage concern
Prioritize DSCR over LTV when assessing debt risk
Require rent comparables to substantiate below-market rent claims
Flag occupancy below 90% as indicator of weak demand rather than opportunity
Include all fees mentioned in verdict sections within the dedicated fees section
Ensure fee calculations in verdict match itemized fees in fee section
Cross-reference all fee mentions between verdict and fee sections for consistency
Add any fees cited in analysis conclusions to the formal fee breakdown
Flag when property/asset management fees exceed 10% combined total
Question fee structures above market rates with 'why' instead of 'can you reduce'
Identify when combined management fees are 3+ percentage points above market standard
Frame fee discussions as justification requests rather than reduction requests
Alert when total management fees exceed 10% threshold for investor review
When recommending caution, identify and explain the single most critical issue with the deal
Flag when GP fees are front-loaded rather than performance-based relative to LP returns
Flag when GP makes market rent claims without supporting data or comps
Flag when going-in DSCR is below 1.0x
Prioritize GP misalignment issues when multiple red flags exist
Highlight unsupported market assumptions as major deal risks
Flag deals with negative initial cash flow coverage ratios
Flag when guaranteed fees exceed GP equity investment by more than 5x
Highlight when multiple acquisition-related fees are charged for the same transaction
Request specific dollar amounts for property and asset management fees for years 1-5
Flag when combined property and asset management fees exceed 6% market standard
Verify waterfall mechanics and preferred return calculation methodology in operating agreement
Review clawback provisions and LP protection mechanisms
Assess LP voting rights on fee increases and major decisions
Evaluate GP removal provisions and LP governance rights
Flag when DSCR falls below 1.20x for heavy scrutiny
State market averages instead of referencing instruction thresholds in flag explanations
Replace threshold language with market benchmark context when explaining flags
Use industry standard comparisons rather than internal rule references in outputs
Remove requests for phase 1 environmental assessments
Do not require audited financials as they are uncommon in LP deals
Flag when exit cap rate is lower than entry cap rate as this indicates negative cap rate compression
Do not flag cap rate expansion where exit cap rate exceeds entry cap rate
Highlight deals with cap rate compression (exit cap < entry cap) as potential concerns
Accept cap rate expansion scenarios as favorable market assumptions
Do not net acquisition fees against GP co-invest when the GP is rolling the acquisition fee into the deal
Flag when acquisition fees are being rolled into deal financing rather than paid upfront by GP
Separate acquisition fee treatment from co-invest calculations when fees are deal-financed
Calculate equity percentage as proportion of total equity stack, not total project cost
Use total equity as denominator when showing equity percentage breakdowns
Exclude debt from equity percentage calculations
Flag deals with projected cap rate compression
Highlight deals with projected cap rate expansion as positive
Alert when exit cap rates are lower than entry cap rates
Flag aggressive cap rate assumptions that rely on market compression
Base sponsor equity share calculations on total equity amount, not total deal capitalization
Include all non-renovation capex when calculating going-in cap rate
Backsolve cap rate calculations to account for immediate capital expenditures
Adjust NOI for required non-renovation capex before determining going-in cap rate
Do not include property management fees in guaranteed fees calculations
Do not flag DSCR above 1.30x as thin coverage
Flag when deferred equity fees are structured as rolled acquisition fees
Identify when sponsor acquisition fees are converted to deemed equity at closing
Highlight deferred equity arrangements that represent rolled sponsor compensation
Mark deals where acquisition fees become sponsor equity instead of cash payment
Do not flag DSCR above 1.30x as thin coverage
Flag when acquisition fees exceed 3% of purchase price
Alert when acquisition fees are below 2% of purchase price as potentially below market
Flag when GP co-invest is less than 5% of total equity investment
Allow acquisition fees larger than GP co-invest if fees are reinvested in the deal rather than distributed to GP
Flag when acquisition fees exceed GP co-invest and are being distributed directly to GP
Verify acquisition fee treatment - whether reinvested or paid out to sponsor
Verify that sponsor co-invest is calculated as percentage of total equity, not percentage of total deal capitalization
Flag when sponsor co-invest percentage appears to be based on total deal value instead of equity portion
Ensure co-invest calculations exclude debt portion from denominator
